author: ABC News YouTube
text:
ABC World News Tonight with David Muir Full Broadcast - May 24, 2026
Jaclyn Lee reports on the state of emergency in California as crews to prevent a chemical container from overheating and possibly leaking or exploding; Lama Hasan has the details on the race to stop the rapidly growing Ebola outbreak, and the mitigation efforts in the United States; and Selina Wang from the White House North Lawn on President Trump’s renewed calls for funding for a White House ballroom; and more on tonight’s broadcast of World News Tonight with David Muir.

00:00 Intro 
02:28 Evacuation orders in California amid efforts to prevent a potential environmental disaster 
04:42 Over 900 Ebola cases amid outbreak in the Congo, as ten countries declared at “high risk”
07:31 Trump renews calls for a White House ballroom amid shooting
09:37 Flash flood threat across several states
10:42 Racing world honors Kyle Busch
11:51 Negotiations over Iran deal continue after change in tone over an end to the war
14:18 Violent crackdown on political protestors in Turkey’s capitol
14:36 At least 19 people injured in a stampede at South Carolina festival
15:45 Dramatic cave rescue in upstate New York 
17:07 Five Chicago police officers struck by car during “teen takeover” 
17:28 Australian man killed by shark attack while spearfishing
17:56 Knicks are one win away from reaching NBA finals 
18:15 Fallen service members honored on Memorial Day observance around the country
18:45 Long belated prom surprise for 100-year-old veteran

ABC World News Tonight with David Muir delivers the news that matters most. Watch to get the latest news stories and headlines from around the world.

author: ABC News YouTube
text:
Memorial Day display places combat boots at cemetery to honor fallen service members
Memorial Day is being observed across the United States, including in Newport, Rhode Island, where thousands of fallen U.S. service members were honored.
 
The memorial features combat boots adorned with flags and placards, with each boot representing a service member killed in action since Sept. 11, 2001.

author: ABC News YouTube
text:
This Week with George Stephanopoulos Full Broadcast - Sunday, May 24, 2026
Reps. Tom Suozzi, D-N.Y., and Brian Fitzpatrick, R-Pa., make the bipartisan case against the anti-weaponization fund. ABC’s John Quiñones reports from Texas’ Rio Grande Valley on the pivotal impact of Latino voters in the Lone Star State. Martha Raddatz speaks about her new book, ‘The Hero Next Door.’
 
00:00 Intro
02:21 Selina Wang reports from the White House after a shots fired incident Saturday evening
05:51 James Longman reports from Tel Aviv with the latest on a potential deal between the U.S. and Iran
07:00 Jay O’Brien reports on the Republican backlash to DOJ’s proposed $1.8 billion anti-weaponization fund
12:00 Reps. Brian Fitzpatrick and Tom Suozzi join “This Week” for an exclusive interview on their bill to effectively block the $1.8 billion fund
20:32 John Quiñones reports from Texas on how Latino voters could play a key role in this year’s midterms
28:10 The Powerhouse Roundtable breaks down the political headlines of the week
40:17 Martha Raddatz speaks about her new book

–––

text:
Republican Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick on the DOJ’s $1.8 billion ‘anti-weaponization fund'
When asked by Jonathan Karl if he fears retribution from President Trump after introducing a bill to stop the ‘anti-weaponization fund,’ Republican Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick said he doesn’t “worry about any outside criticism.”


“Everybody's got a job to do. Everybody's right and free to speak their mind.”
